channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: CCPD Virtual Appointments Notice
item.description: Due to ongoing campus construction, the Center for Career and Professional Development (CCPD) will be offering virtual appointments only at this time. If you require any special accommodations or need to meet on campus, please get in touch with us at ccpd@rpi.edu, and we will work with you to arrange an in-person option. To schedule a virtual appointment with one of our career counselors, please log in to Handshake. We appreciate your understanding and apologize for any inconvenience.
item.author: Students

item.title: Agentic AI Hackathon with OpenClaw (Prizes and pizza!)
item.description: Join us on Saturday, June 6th, for a beginner-friendly hackathon exploring AI agents on OpenClaw - an open-source platform for building, running, and orchestrating AI agents that can reason through problems, use tools, and complete multi-step tasks, all using natural language. This event is hosted by Tetherless World Constellation in collaboration with GRAIL (https://grailai.io). Pizza and prizes included. Registration is required.
item.author: Students

item.title: RPI LGTBQ+ Taskforce & EMPAC Pride Celebration!
item.description: Get ready to celebrate love, identity, and community—we’re thrilled to invite you to our Pride Preparation Party on Friday, May 29th and to march with us in the Capital Pride Parade on Sunday, June 14th! Please fill out this form and share if you plan to join!
item.author: Community

item.title: Add a Little Whimsy to Your Study Routine: Silent Reads @ Folsom Library
item.description: Join RPI Libraries for Silent Reads — a relaxed, low-pressure gathering where people read together in silence. No discussions, no pressure, just a calm space to settle in with a good book or some coursework. Bring anything you like to read: a graphic novel, an audiobook, a research article, or that novel you’ve been meaning to start for months. Not sure what to bring? Check out our new books (we are a library after all). Free snacks and beverages provided. Drop in, recharge, and bring a friend.
item.author: Community

item.title: Materials Research Center Parking Lot Closure
item.description: On Thursday, May 28, 2026, the Materials Research Center (MRC) parking lot, located on the loading dock side of the building, will be closed and unavailable for parking. A crane will be on-site to complete the installation of a new cooling tower at the Cogswell Chiller Plant.
item.author: Community

item.title: RPI at Work Hosts Systems Engineering Review on May 29
item.description: Date: Friday, May 29, 2026 12:00 PM - 12:30 PM - This 30-minute information session is hosted by Dean Aric Krause, Ph.D., and will focus on the RPI at Work Systems Engineering graduate degree. The degree is digitally delivered, flexible, and project-based to prepare decision-makers with broader and deeper capabilities in leading dynamic organizations. The session will highlight program details and how you can select graduate certificates relevant to your work to complete your graduate degree.
item.author: Community

item.title: Make the Most of Your CDPHP Fitness Reimbursement – Capital Region Basketball Camp at RPI
item.description: Registration is open for The Capital Region Basketball Camp for youth at RPI, led by Mark Gilbride, Head Men’s Basketball Coach. CDPHP members can submit a fitness reimbursement request via their member account online.
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Spring 2026 End of Term Student Course Evaluations: Reviewing Evaluation Results (Training for Instructors and TAs) Webinar
item.description: Date: Thursday, May 28, 2026 11:00 AM - 12:00 PM - This webinar is for instructors and TA’s that would like to understand or have a refresher of the various ways to review their student course evaluations in HelioCampus.
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Summer II 2026 End of Term Course Surveys in HelioCampus: Adding Additional Questions to a Course Section (Faculty/Instructor Training)
item.description: Date: Tuesday, June 2, 2026 11:30 AM - 12:00 PM - This webinar offers faculty/instructors of course sections to add additional questions to their student end of term course evaluations. This is an optional process and only needed if instructors wanted to add other questions than what is on the institute course evaluation. Please click the link in the See section to register for the session.
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Division of Human Resources Professional Development Seminar: “Supervisory Essentials” June 10, 2026
item.description: In this seminar, Managers and Supervisors will learn from each other and our Facilitators about important topics such as the Employee Value Proposition and Employee Experience, the six fundamental Leadership competencies at Rensselaer, and experience a “day in the life” of a Supervisor. In addition, the importance of managing employee performance, providing both rewards and recognition, employment law fundamentals and tools for building team capacity will be explored. For more information…
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Join HR Virtual Office Hours - RPI MAX Annual Performance Evaluations
item.description:
Human Resources invites managers and staff to attend upcoming Virtual HR Office Hours to answer questions regarding the RPI MAX Annual Staff Performance Evaluation process. Stop in for help with PageUp navigation, the evaluation process, timelines, and related topics. We look forward to connecting with you!

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
